subject: Mathematics
teacherActions: Present two phone plans with different monthly fees and per-minute costs.
Ask students to record values in a table before introducing equations.
Guide students to graph the data points.
Prompt discussion: What does the starting value represent? What does the rate show?
Introduce slope and intercept language after student discovery.
studentActions: Explain findings verbally before writing equations
frameworkLang: Instruction engages students in inquiry and conceptual understanding
Teaching connects learning to real-life and authentic contexts

subject: Science
teacherActions: Show an image of a missing predator species.
Ask students to predict the impact on the ecosystem.
Run a simple simulation or card-based food web activity.
Pause for observation and note changes.
Facilitate explanation using scientific vocabulary.
frameworkLang: Students actively investigate questions using evidence

subject: English
teacherActions: Cut a narrative text into short sections.
Model one inference aloud using evidence.
Give groups one text strip at a time.
Ask: What can we infer? What supports it?
Collect responses using a class inference chart.
studentActions: Justify inferences verbally and in writing
frameworkLang: Lessons meet the needs of diverse learners

subject: History
teacherActions: Provide images and fact cards.
Assign categories (home, food, work, religion).
Have students place items on a shared timeline wall.
Ask students to justify placement choices.
frameworkLang: Curriculum enables understanding of concepts over time
Learning experiences are engaging and structured

subject: Arabic
teacherActions: Display model paragraph with highlighted connectors.
Provide sentence frames and vocabulary lists.
Guide whole-class paragraph construction.
Gradually remove supports.
frameworkLang: Teaching strategies ensure access for all learners
Students progress from supported to independent learning

subject: Science
teacherActions: Present a practical challenge (safe ramp design).
Ask groups to predict outcomes using formulas.
Allow testing using simulations or models.
Require written justification of decisions.
frameworkLang: Students apply learning to unfamiliar contexts
Assessment tasks promote reasoning and justification
Teaching encourages critical and analytical thinking
